description: Habit Tracking System - Backend: HabitAgent, routes, orchestrator integration, and Task Completion via Chat (planner update)
steps: 1. Update backend/app/db/models.py: add target_days field (int, default 7) to Habit model for frequency tracking
2. Update backend/app/db/repositories.py: add delete method to HabitRepository, and a get_by_name_and_user method for fuzzy matching habits by name

3. Create backend/app/agents/habits.py - HabitAgent inheriting from AgentBase with: name='habits', actions: create_habit, list_habits, check_in, delete_habit. Uses self.generate() with a HABIT_PROMPT to classify user intent. Streak logic: consecutive days meeting goal resets on miss unless frequency allows gaps. Uses HabitRepository. Uses timectx for IST timezone.

4. Update backend/app/agents/orchestrator.py SYSTEM_PROMPT: add 'habits' to Available agents list with description, add routing rules for habit-related phrases ('habits', 'streak', 'check in', 'went to gym', 'done with X habit', 'how are my habits') to route to habits agent. Also add routing for completion phrases ('I'm done with X', 'finished X', 'completed X', 'mark X as done') to route to planner.

5. Update backend/app/agents/planner.py PLANNER_PROMPT: add 'complete_task' action to ACTION SELECTION RULES. When user says 'I finished X' / 'done with X' / 'mark X as done' -> action: 'complete_task'. The execute() method handles this action by listing tasks, finding the matching one, calling complete_task MCP tool, and returning a satisfying confirmation message.

6. Update backend/app/main.py: import and register HabitAgent in lifespan (after other agents). Add routes: GET /api/habits?auth_token=... and POST /api/habits/checkin (body: {auth_token, habit_id}). Add POST /api/habits (body: {auth_token, name, frequency, target_days}) for creating habits from frontend. Add DELETE /api/habits/{habit_id}?auth_token=...

7. Add backend/tests/test_habits.py: test HabitAgent create, list, check_in, delete actions using mock pattern from test_agents.py. Test planner complete_task action. At least 5 test cases covering: create habit, list habits, check-in (streak increment), delete habit, planner complete_task.
8. Verify: python -c 'import app.main' passes, python -m pytest tests/ -v passes with new tests
acceptance_criteria: python -c 'import app.main' passes with HabitAgent registered
GET /api/habits returns habits for a user
POST /api/habits/checkin records completion and updates streak
Orchestrator routes habit-related phrases to habits agent
Orchestrator routes completion phrases to planner
Planner handles complete_task action by calling MCP complete_task tool
python -m pytest tests/ -v passes including new test_habits.py tests
findings: All 107 tests pass (93 original + 14 new). python -c 'import app.main' works. No issues encountered. The complete_task MCP tool takes auth_token and task_id. The planner lists tasks first, matches by title substring, then calls complete_task.
